lines.target: Some more examples of “ make” and “do”:
lines.base: Algunos ejemplos más de make y do:

lines.target: He is making* a film about living conditions in a peasant village.
lines.base: Está haciendo una película sobre las condiciones de vida en un pueblo campesino.
lines.pronunciation: .. kéndishéns... pesánt... — 3 ... dchouks. — 4... bisi... prepáreishens... — 5... mauz.. — 6... lá8f! — 8 ... sákssilds... angri. — 9 ... maind... klous. — 10 uotevé ... nauáceis... — 11 missis richárds guests ... admairin... staft shaark... mauntit... — 12 ... koot... praud oonér. — 14 ... hoostess.

lines.target: | needed his help, but all he could do* was make jokes.
lines.base: Necesité su ayuda, pero todo lo que pudo hacer fue hacer chistes.

lines.target: They are busy making preparations for their holiday.
lines.base: Están ocupados haciendo [los] preparativos de sus vacaciones.

lines.target: The smell of good cooking makes my mouth water.
lines.base: El olor de la buena cocina me hace la boca agua.

lines.target: You're going* to marry a millionaire? Don't make me laugh!
lines.base: ¿ Vas a casarte con un millonario? j No me hagas reír!

lines.target: | have no coffee, you'll have to make do with tea.
lines.base: No tengo café, tienes que conformarte con té.

lines.target: He always succeeds in making me angry.
Please make up your mind, we don't have very much time and the shop is going to close.
lines.base: Por favor, decidase, no tenemos mucho tiempo y la tienda va a cerrar.

lines.target: Whatever you look at nowadays, you will see": “ Made in Hong Kong ”.
lines.base: Mires lo que mires hoy día, verás: « Hecho en Hong Kong ».

lines.target: Mrs Richard's guests were admiring a large stuffed shark that was mounted on her wall.
lines.base: Los invitados de la Sra. Richard admiraban un gran tiburón disecado que estaba colgado (fijado) en la pared.

lines.target: My husband and I caught* it on a fishing trip, said* the proud owner.
lines.base: Mi marido y yo lo cazamos en un viaje de pesca, dijo la orgullosa propietaria.

lines.target: What is it stuffed with? asked one lady.
lines.base: ¿Con que está relleno ? preguntó una señora.

They are busy making preparations for their holiday.
| have no coffee, you'll have to make do with tea. (3)
He always succeeds in making me angry.
Mrs Richard's guests were admiring a large stuffed shark that was mounted on her wall. (5)
What is it stuffed with? asked one lady.
